documents.content: In Years 7 and 8 Health and Physical Education, under Movement and Physical Activity (Moving our body), students learn to: Use feedback to improve body control and coordination when performing specialised movement skills in a variety of situations

In Years 9 and 10 Health and Physical Education, under Personal, Social and Community Health (Being healthy, safe and active), students learn to: Evaluate factors that shape identities and critically analyse how individuals impact the identities of others

In Years 9 and 10 Health and Physical Education, under Personal, Social and Community Health (Being healthy, safe and active), students learn to: Examine the impact of changes and transitions on relationships

In Years 9 and 10 Health and Physical Education, under Personal, Social and Community Health (Being healthy, safe and active), students learn to: Plan, rehearse and evaluate options (including CPR and first aid) for managing situations where their own or others’ health, safety and wellbeing may be at short or long term risk

In Years 9 and 10 Health and Physical Education, under Personal, Social and Community Health (Being healthy, safe and active), students learn to: Propose, practise and evaluate responses in situations where external influences may impact on their ability to make healthy and safe choices

In Years 9 and 10 Health and Physical Education, under Personal, Social and Community Health (Communicating and interacting for health and wellbeing), students learn to: Investigate how empathy and ethical decision making contribute to respectful relationships
